buildVerification.warningDetails: All warnings are pre-existing across multiple blocks (react-hooks/exhaustive-deps). EventHero-specific warnings (lines 176) unchanged from prior run — same pattern as PostHero, not a regression.

syncVerification.rootWrapperWarning: Known false-positive (InspectorControls vs section) — same as PostHero, pre-existing.

FQA-001.issue: PHP native date() used for date comparison — should use wp_date() for site-timezone consistency
evidence: EventHero.php line 49: wp_date('Y-m-d', $start_ts) used for all-day same-day check
EventHero.php line 50: wp_date('Y-m-d', $end_ts) used in ISO interval suffix
EventHero.php line 63: wp_date('Y-m-d', $start_ts) === wp_date('Y-m-d', $end_ts) for same-day timed check
EventHero.php line 64/68: wp_date('Y-m-d\TH:i', ...) used for ISO datetime attribute values
No remaining bare date() calls in the date logic block — confirmed by code review

FQA-002.issue:
Date/time rendered in plain

tags, no element for machine readability

php.newVariable: $date_time_iso built with wp_date() — ISO 8601 interval format for ranges (e.g. '2026-02-20T18:00/2026-02-20T21:00'), date-only for all-day events
php.element: EventHero.php line 162: <time datetime="<?php echo esc_attr($date_time_iso); ?>"> wraps display text — correctly escaped with esc_attr()
php.guardedBy: if ($date_time_display) conditional at line 160 — <time> only renders when date is available

tsx.newHelper: buildIsoDatetime() function (lines 28–57) generates ISO datetime string covering all four cases: all-day single, all-day multi-day, timed single, timed range
tsx.element: EventHero.tsx line 306: <time dateTime={dateTimeIso || undefined}> — undefined when no startDate avoids empty datetime="" attribute
tsx.isoFormat: Interval notation with '/' separator used for ranges — valid HTML datetime attribute format per spec

notes: No new issues introduced by the fixes
All existing passing checks still pass
FQA-003 (TSX always renders date-time line; PHP guards with if) remains a noted item — not a regression, not worsened by these fixes
FQA-004 (lower group div always renders) unchanged — note-level, no action needed

observations: All 6 block instances render correctly at 1440px — gradient, hero layout, and title all intact
No visual regressions from the PHP/TSX changes
Rendering is identical to prior QA run — fixes were purely structural/semantic (wp_date, time element), not visual

openIssues.description: TSX always renders the date-time <time> element (with 'Event Date' placeholder when no startDate). PHP guards with if($date_time_display). Minor editor-vs-frontend discrepancy, harmless in practice since TEC always populates _EventStartDate.
Lower group <div class='mt-12 flex flex-col gap-1'> always renders even when all children are absent. Not a real-world issue.
